liftedFindings.title: `reviews[X].waived_hash` silently stores a *different node's* hash than its name implies
Git tracked check depends on the caller’s cwd, not the checked file’s repo
`pc asset add` silently discards `--media` on the re-add path, and no verb can ever correct a recorded media type
`asset add` deletes the local copy after `has()` proves only key presence, not byte integrity
`pc explain --json` drops the structured cause identity
The `PC_REQUIRE_DOCKER` enforcement gate is asserted to be set by CI, but nothing in the repo sets it
The Docker requirement is only read, not wired into any required test command
The escape test creates the escaped file itself before asserting it does not exist
The offline-by-construction proof never checks its own walk for holes — only the ROOT_FILES walk does
Fetched assets are materialized into a flat directory keyed only by basename, so two inputs sharing a filename clobber each other after verification
Derived input resolution trusts ledger paths outside the episode
Case 9 cannot fail for the reason it claims — its fixture is Case 10's fixture, so it passes on the both-authored-and-derived refusal regardless of whether `follows`-on-derived is checked at all
Rebuild path is mocked, so the main FR-022a guard cannot catch a real build regression
`pc status` half of the FR-025 contrast cannot detect the probe it claims to guard against
`resolveAuthored` reports a directory as `{ kind: 'file' }`, silently bypassing the FR-026 size guard
S3 `put` treats a corrupt object key as durable storage
The path-escape refinement is fixtured only on leading `../` and leading `/` — interior traversal and the empty path are unenumerated channels
`RelativePathSchema` test enshrines a lexical-containment claim that is false under symlinks
Chunk 95355b61788b5b2e declares 29 files in scope but renders diffs for only 4
The "defaults to the current directory" test never omits `--episode` — the default-resolution path is entirely untested
Validation records can clobber a newer artifact record
Waivers Can Transfer Across A Different `follows` Target With The Same Hash
Symlinked outputs defeat both the containment check and the undeclared-file walk
`pc validate` on a modified artifact records a verdict about bytes that are not on disk
Recursive Output Reconciliation Silently Treats Filesystem Errors As Empty Output
Unconstrained `IdentitySchema` lets a manifest identity escape `dist/` and drive a recursive delete
`hashPath` follows symlinks with `stat`, so the tree walker's symlink refusal is bypassed at the top level
`writeLedger` rewrites the committed source of truth non-atomically
`renderStatus` and `toNodeJson` disagree on how "no producer drift" is represented; a `null` crashes the human renderer
A waiver pins the hash observed at write time, not the hash the human actually reviewed — the TOCTOU window silently launders unreviewed content
The output declaration is never compared against the record — a changed `output.path` is a false-clean
The atomic-ingest test proves only one arm of the split it claims to close
Integration global setup still races contract builds on shared dist
`cachedStore.put` write-through to the authoritative store is never verified — the test that claims to cover it is satisfied entirely by the cache
Non-input causes fall back to falsely causal input chains
